articles.title: Concerned SoCal woman reveals terrifying fallout from registering her pet Boxer to vote
articles.description: Laura Yourex thought she was doing the right thing when she registered her dog, Maya, to vote during the 2020 election — exposing vulnerabilities of a system that politicians have long warned about, but officials have done little to fix.

articles.title: Trump faces 'calamitous' situation as his 'political charm' starts to backfire: author
articles.description: An author who has written four books about President Donald Trump said on Saturday that the president's "political charm" is starting to backfire on the administration. For years, part of Trump's appeal to voters has been that he does not course-correct when things go sideways, journalist Michael Wolff argued in a new episode of "Inside Trump's Head," a podcast he co-hosts with Joanna Coles of The Daily Beast. However, that strategy appears to be failing him as the administration and the Republican Party limp toward the 2026 midterms. "Course correcting is the absolute opposite of what he does. Trump is strictly a double-down guy," Wolff said. "It's part of his political charm in its way. 'I don't pander, I don't give a s---. I just do what I'm going to do.' And for various points that has been impressive, even charming, to lots of people." "But at various other points, it's calamitous, and I think we're at one of those points," he added. Wolff noted that Trump seems to have stopped discussing issues that matter to the MAGA base in the White House, citing conversations he'd had over the last week. For instance, Wolff said Trump still measures the economy's success by stock market performance, which doesn't resonate with many MAGA voters. He's also been raging about the Federal Reserve and the White House Ballroom, two other issues that don't appeal to MAGA, he said. "Everybody around him, their response is frustration, but I think they have to convert that into patience because you can't tell him, 'Hey, we're really heading for disaster here.' They are heading for disaster. No one can say that."

articles.title: North Korea launches ballistic missiles toward sea
articles.description: South Korea says North Korea has launched multiple ballistic missiles toward the North’s eastern waters

articles.title: Hundreds show up to Portland clown march, in silly day of joyous protest
articles.description: “We will keep finding ridiculous ways to disrupt the status quo,” said Raggs, the lead clown.

articles.title: Iran's Qalibaf Cites 'Progress' But Says 'Many Gaps Remain,' As Trump Rejects 'Blackmail'
articles.description: Iran's powerful parliament speaker said there was "progress" in talks with Washington but that "fundamental" differences remain, while US President Donald Trump rejected what he called "blackmail" by Tehran after it again closed the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Protest against AI Data Centre takes place at Sask. legislative building
articles.description: Ahead of a key meeting on Monday, Regina citizens took to the Saskatchewan legislative building to protest the continuation of the new AI Data Centre.

articles.title: Sen. Elizabeth Warren rallies with Graham Platner in Maine's Democratic Senate primary
articles.description: At a packed rally in Maine, Sen. Elizabeth Warren, D-Mass., called on voters to support political newcomer Graham Platner in a closely watched Democratic primary to challenge longtime Republican Sen. Susan Collins.

articles.title: Regina residents rally against proposed AI data centre before RM of Sherwood council vote
articles.description: Nearly 200 people gathered at the legislature in Regina Saturday to protest the lack of consultation around Bell Canada's planned AI data centre, set to be built in Sherwood. The rural municipality's council is set to vote on the AI centre's development agreement on Monday.
